title: Tournament Entry Payment System with ZAP UPI API

requirements.text: Create a `tournament_entries` stable data structure in the Motoko backend actor to store tournament entry records with fields: id (auto-incremented Nat), name (Text), game_id (Text), payment_id (Text), status (Text, e.g. 'PENDING' or 'CONFIRMED'), and created_at (Int timestamp). Use a stable var array or HashMap keyed by id.

quotes: Create database table: tournament_entries Fields: id (auto), name, game_id, payment_id, status, created_at

acceptanceCriteria: A stable data structure for tournament_entries exists in backend/main.mo
Each entry stores id, name, game_id, payment_id, status, and created_at
IDs are auto-incremented starting from 1

requirements.text: Add a `createOrder` update function to the Motoko backend actor that: (1) accepts name (Text) and gameId (Text), (2) checks if the current confirmed+pending entry count is >= 50 and returns an error if so, (3) makes an outgoing HTTP call to the ZAP UPI API to create a payment order with amount 5000, currency INR, using the ZAP API key in the Authorization header (stored as a canister environment constant, never exposed to frontend), (4) parses the returned payment URL from the ZAP API response, (5) creates a PENDING entry in tournament_entries, and (6) returns the payment URL to the caller.

quotes: Create a backend endpoint: POST /create-order
Accept name and gameId
Call ZAP API to create payment order
Use API key in Authorization header
Currency: INR
Return payment URL to frontend
Max players: 50
If count >= 50, block new order creation

acceptanceCriteria: createOrder function rejects with an error message when confirmed player count >= 50
createOrder calls ZAP API with amount 5000, currency INR, and Authorization header containing the API key
A PENDING entry is created in tournament_entries upon successful order creation
The payment URL returned by ZAP API is returned to the frontend caller
ZAP API key is defined as a canister-side constant and never sent to the frontend

requirements.text: Add a `handleWebhook` update function to the Motoko backend actor that: (1) accepts the raw request body (Text) and a ZAP-Signature header value (Text), (2) verifies the HMAC SHA256 signature using the stored ZAP Secret Key constant against the raw body, (3) if verification fails returns an error, (4) if payment status in the payload equals 'SUCCESS', finds the matching tournament_entry by paymentId, updates status to 'CONFIRMED', saves player name, game_id, and payment_id, (5) returns `{ success: true }` on success.

quotes: Create webhook endpoint: POST /webhook
Verify ZAP signature using HMAC SHA256 with my Secret Key
If payment status = SUCCESS: Save player name, Save gameId, Save paymentId, Mark status as CONFIRMED
Return 200 OK
Return JSON: { success: true }

acceptanceCriteria: handleWebhook rejects requests with invalid HMAC SHA256 signatures
Entries with matching paymentId are updated to status CONFIRMED when payload contains SUCCESS
The function returns a success indicator on valid, successful webhook processing
ZAP Secret Key is stored only on the backend, never exposed to the frontend

requirements.text: Store the ZAP API key and ZAP Secret Key as canister-level private constants in backend/main.mo (not in frontend code, not passed through query parameters). Add a query function `getEntryCount` returning the current number of tournament entries, and a query function `getTournamentEntries` (admin-only) returning the full list of entries.

quotes: Do not expose secret key in frontend
Use environment variables for API and Secret key

acceptanceCriteria: ZAP API key and Secret Key are defined only in backend/main.mo as private constants
No key values appear in any frontend file
getEntryCount query is publicly accessible
getTournamentEntries query is restricted to the admin principal

requirements.text: Create a Tournament Entry / Payment page in the frontend (e.g. `frontend/src/pages/TournamentEntry.tsx`) with a form collecting player name and Free Fire Game ID. On submit, call the backend `createOrder` function and redirect the user to the returned ZAP payment URL. Show an error if slots are full (count >= 50). Display current slot availability (e.g. 'X / 50 slots filled'). Style consistently with the existing Free Fire battle theme (GamingCard, GamingButton components, ff-orange/ff-cyan palette).

quotes: Accept name and gameId
Return payment URL to frontend
If count >= 50, block new order creation

acceptanceCriteria: Form collects name and gameId fields with validation
Slot count is fetched and displayed; form is disabled when slots >= 50
Successful order creation redirects user to the ZAP payment URL
Error states (full slots, API failure) are shown with user-friendly messages in English
Page uses GamingCard and GamingButton components and matches the Free Fire battle theme

requirements.text: Add a route for the Tournament Entry page to `frontend/src/App.tsx` (e.g. `/tournament-entry`) and add a navigation link to it in `frontend/src/components/layout/Navigation.tsx` visible to authenticated users.

quotes: Create a secure tournament entry payment system

acceptanceCriteria: Route /tournament-entry renders the TournamentEntry page
Navigation bar shows a 'Tournament Entry' link for authenticated users
Routing integrates cleanly with the existing TanStack Router setup in App.tsx

requirements.text: Add a section to the existing Admin Dashboard page (`frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboard.tsx`) that displays the list of tournament entries (name, game_id, payment_id, status, created_at) fetched via the admin-only `getTournamentEntries` backend query. Show total confirmed entries and total slot usage.

quotes: tournament_entries Fields: id (auto), name, game_id, payment_id, status, created_at

acceptanceCriteria: Admin Dashboard shows a table of tournament entries with all fields
Total confirmed entries and slot usage (X/50) are displayed
Data is fetched using React Query and the existing admin actor pattern
Section is only visible to authenticated admin users

constraints: ZAP API key and Secret Key must never appear in any frontend file or be returned by any public query
All Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo (single-actor architecture)
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
HMAC SHA256 signature verification must be performed on the backend only
Slot limit of 50 max players must be enforced on the backend, not just the frontend
nonGoals: Implementing a custom payment gateway other than ZAP UPI API
Real-time WebSocket notifications for payment status
Email or SMS confirmation messages to players
Stripe or any non-UPI payment method
Exposing raw webhook payloads to the frontend
Multi-tournament support (single tournament slot pool only)
